techniques.technique_name: Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Supply Chain
techniques.evidence_quote: Compromised developer machine led to malicious JavaScript being injected into the Safe{Wallet} supply chain
Malicious JavaScript via S3 bucket write access
Malicious JavaScript injected into Safe{Wallet} frontend served to users
Developer machine compromise yielded credentials enabling S3 bucket write access

techniques.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API

techniques.evidence_quote: Compromised developer machine — credentials/secrets harvested to enable S3 write
Attacker identified and targeted the Safe{Wallet} S3 bucket hosting frontend assets
Pivot from compromised developer machine to AWS S3 environment
Malicious JavaScript intercepted transaction signing data in the Bybit web frontend
Malicious JavaScript exfiltrated signing data/transaction details to attacker-controlled infrastructure

attack_chain.description: Threat actor compromised a Safe{Wallet} developer's machine, likely via spearphishing or social engineering, establishing an initial foothold on the developer endpoint.

With access to the developer machine, the attacker harvested AWS credentials (access keys, session tokens, or stored secrets) that had write permissions to the Safe{Wallet} S3 bucket hosting frontend JavaScript assets.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Compromised developer machine ... Malicious JavaScript via S3 bucket write access

attack_chain.description: Using the harvested developer credentials, the attacker enumerated the AWS environment to identify the specific S3 bucket serving Safe{Wallet} frontend JavaScript to end-users.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Malicious JavaScript via S3 bucket write access

attack_chain.technique_name: Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_chain.description: The attacker wrote malicious JavaScript to the Safe{Wallet} S3 bucket, poisoning the frontend JavaScript bundle served to all users of the Safe{Wallet} web application, including Bybit's operators.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Escalation Vectors: Malicious JavaScript via S3 bucket write access

attack_chain.description: The malicious JavaScript executed in the browsers of Bybit signers when they loaded the Safe{Wallet} interface, enabling the attacker to intercept and manipulate transaction signing operations in real time.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Malicious JavaScript via S3 bucket write access ... $1.5 Billion dollars stolen

attack_chain.description: The injected JavaScript silently manipulated transaction parameters (destination address and amounts) presented to Bybit signers during the signing ceremony, causing signers to unknowingly approve a fraudulent transaction transferring ~$1.5B in assets to attacker-controlled wallets.

With the manipulated transaction signed and broadcast by legitimate Bybit signers, approximately $1.5 billion in cryptocurrency was transferred to wallets controlled by the threat actor (attributed to Lazarus Group / DPRK).

initial_access_vector: Developer workstation compromise (likely spearphishing/social engineering) targeting a Safe{Wallet} developer, yielding AWS credentials with S3 write access to the frontend asset bucket
persistence_mechanism: Malicious JavaScript persisted in the Safe{Wallet} S3 bucket, executing in every user's browser session until detected and remediated
privilege_escalation: Harvested developer AWS credentials granted direct S3 write access to production frontend bucket, requiring no additional privilege escalation within AWS
